# Kobol

> Kobol (with a K) is a modern, COBOL-inspired programming language for the JVM. It is **inspired by COBOL — not COBOL**: a new, independent language with its own syntax, compiler, and runtime. It is not a COBOL dialect, is not source-compatible with COBOL, and is not affiliated with or endorsed by any COBOL vendor or standards body.

Kobol keeps the qualities that made COBOL durable — English-like readability, data-first structure, and exact decimal arithmetic — and rebuilds them on a modern JVM foundation (Java 21 virtual threads, sealed classes, pattern matching). It compiles to JVM bytecode and offers full Java interop, so any Java class is one `IMPORT` away. Existing COBOL programs do not run as-is — they must be refitted and rewritten — but migrating from COBOL to Kobol is far gentler than rewriting in a terse, symbol-heavy language.

## Key facts

- Name: Kobol (spelled with a K). Pronounced like "cobalt" without the "t".
- Category: programming language, JVM-hosted, business/back-office logic.
- Runtime: JVM bytecode + a Java transpiler; runs anywhere Java runs. No mainframe required.
- Compiler: written in Kotlin; emits bytecode via ASM.
- Hallmarks: exact decimal/MONEY arithmetic (BigDecimal), record-oriented batch I/O, `CONCURRENT` blocks on virtual threads, declarative `VALIDATE`, built-in `TEST` blocks, a versioned module system, and Script mode (no boilerplate for small programs).
- License: Apache-2.0.

## Documentation

- [Language Specification](https://github.com/kobol-lang/kobol/blob/main/docs/LANGUAGE_SPEC.md): complete grammar, type system, and compiler pipeline reference.
- [README](https://github.com/kobol-lang/kobol/blob/main/README.md): overview, design principles, install and usage.
- [Examples](https://github.com/kobol-lang/kobol/tree/main/examples): runnable `.kbl` programs, including Java-interop demos.

## Source

- [Source repository](https://github.com/kobol-lang/kobol)
- [VS Code extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=kobol-lang.kobol-language-support)
- Website: https://kobol-lang.org/
